mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 1.0% signals monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:current FEDFUNDS at 0.15% indicates deep accommodation regime
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle with rates near zero lower bound
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in low-rate periods (FEDFUNDS < 1.0%) do not significantly exceed baseline returns (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate above 7.5% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations decline.
evidence_refs: fact:current UNRATE at 7.7% exceeds trigger threshold
regime_hint: labor market weakness or post-shock recovery phase
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE > 7.5%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in real retail sales (RSAFS) combined with declining housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand and consumer confidence, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as cyclical earnings forecasts are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 2% month-over-month AND HOUST falls below 900k units annualized
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 890k units near trigger threshold
regime_hint: late-cycle slowdown or early recession warning
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of co-triggered instances (both RSAFS and HOUST conditions met), or joint signal Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) below 99.5 index points signals supply-side weakness and capacity constraints that compress profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as near-term earnings surprises turn negative.
evidence_refs: fact:current INDPRO at 99.4965 is below trigger threshold
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where INDPRO < 99.5, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerating below 50k new jobs per month combined with rising inflation (CPIAUCSL > 233) signals stagflationary pressure that reduces real wage growth and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: stagflation or high-inflation labor market tightness
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of co-triggered instances, or joint signal Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) expanding above 13,600 billion chained dollars signals robust economic expansion that supports equity earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as consensus earnings estimates are raised.
evidence_refs: fact:current GDPC1 at 13,656.8 billion indicates expansion
regime_hint: mid-to-late cycle expansion with positive growth momentum
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of expansion periods (GDPC1 > 13,600), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL rising above 233 combined with elevated federal funds rate above 0.5% signals monetary tightening that increases equity discount rates and reduces forward earnings multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 232.77 and FEDFUNDS at 0.15% show mixed signals
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of co-triggered instances, or joint signal Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth combined with payroll employment (PAYEMS) expansion above 135,000k persons signals robust supply-side capacity and labor availability that supports margin expansion and productivity gains, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 135,046k near threshold, INDPRO at 99.4965 below threshold
regime_hint: supply-side expansion with tight labor markets
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 55% of co-triggered instances, or joint signal Rank IC < 0.04 over any 1-year rolling window.
